title: Stacked Changes, Maintainer Workflows, and Integration Queues · Changed-History Lab
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer stacked changes, maintainer workflows, and integration queues into a new stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.
Use range-diff and interdiff-style evidence to explain rerolls, reordered commits, changed commit messages, and semantic deltas between series.
Compare topic-branch, integration-branch, subsystem, patch-flow, merge-queue, and release-train workflows by authority and recovery cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G70 is a lab in a disposable repository. the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies. The learner must predict old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible, run git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2, explain one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent, test this changed condition: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-collaboration-policy-defense-81zf2e" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Collaboration Policy Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-collaboration-policy-defense-81zf2e
# scenario-key: trace-81zf2e probe-e2fz18 boundary-zf2e81 owner-81zf2ex fault-81zf2ez
# before-ref: main=a11ce81 candidate=b00b12e
# hypothesis: the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies
git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2
# expected: old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible
# observation: one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent
# changed-case: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree
# after-ref: main=a11ce81 candidate=c0ffeee
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published
# transfer-limit: provider merge queues need separate event, identity, permission, and tested-revision verification
